metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit preservation instruction to the Scope or AC: the `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation (README.md line 542, required by the MCP Registry for PyPI-ownership verification per `docs/releasing.md`) must be retained in README.md after the MCP catalog is trimmed — add an AC or scope note specifying this line must survive the edit.
findings.finding: The `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation at README.md line 542 sits inside the `## MCP server` section that this plan will trim. `docs/releasing.md` (line 157) explicitly states this annotation 'lives in README.md = the PyPI long description; the registry greps it to verify package ownership' — losing it would silently break MCP Registry publish. The plan's scope says to replace 'volatile catalogs' with a pointer+link but gives no instruction to preserve or relocate this annotation.
findings.location: Scope section; README.md lines 525–542 (`## MCP server` section containing `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` at line 542)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope or a new AC: 'The `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` registry annotation (currently at README.md line 542) MUST be kept in README.md — it is required for MCP Registry publish ownership verification (see `docs/releasing.md`). Move it to the shortened `## MCP server` section or the License section; do not delete it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly preserve the `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation line (README.md:542) in the post-edit README, documenting in the plan that this is a machine-read PyPI long-description marker required by the MCP Registry (`docs/releasing.md:157`) and must not be removed along with the `## MCP server` catalog content.
findings.finding: The `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation at README.md:542 is a functional machine-read marker the MCP Registry uses to verify PyPI package ownership (docs/releasing.md:157 confirms: 'The PyPI release must carry the `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation (it lives in `README.md` = the PyPI long description; the registry greps it to verify package ownership)'). This annotation sits inside the `## MCP server` section that the plan targets for removal/replacement. The plan does not enumerate this annotation as a constraint that must survive the edit, leaving an executor unaware that silently dropping it would break MCP Registry re-publish.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the Scope section: 'The bare `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` line (README.md:542) is a machine-read MCP Registry ownership marker and must be retained verbatim in the post-edit README even if the surrounding `## MCP server` catalog is replaced with a pointer link.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note in the Scope or a new AC that the `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation (README.md line 542, inside the `## MCP server` section) MUST be retained verbatim in README.md after the trim — and add a verification command: `grep -qF 'mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar' README.md || echo 'MISSING MCP ANNOTATION'`.
findings.finding: The plan's mechanism of trimming the `## MCP server` catalog section risks silently deleting the `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation (README.md line 542) that the MCP registry greps from the PyPI long description to verify package ownership. If this annotation is removed, future PyPI releases will fail the MCP registry publish step. No AC checks for the annotation's survival.
findings.location: Scope section / AC (no AC covers annotation survival) / README.md line 542 / docs/releasing.md lines 157-159
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: `- [ ] The MCP registry ownership annotation is preserved — `grep -qF 'mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar' README.md || echo 'MISSING MCP ANNOTATION'` prints nothing. (This annotation is grepped by the MCP registry from the PyPI long description to verify package ownership; removing it breaks future releases — see docs/releasing.md.)`
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the routing-section heading (e.g. `'## Routing'` or whatever heading it carries in the current README) to the AC #3 grep list so all four scope-named categories (what/why, install, quickstart, routing) have a deterministic preservation check.
findings.finding: The Scope explicitly names 'routing' as front-door content to preserve ('routing links'), but AC #3's heading grep checks omit any routing heading, leaving no deterministic proof that a routing section is not accidentally dropped.
findings.suggested_fix: Inspect the current README for the heading that covers routing/dispatch (e.g. `## Routing` or `## How routing works`) and append `'## <that heading>'` to the AC #3 for-loop list so every scope-named section is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-flight assertion to the AC (or a note to Scope) confirming `docs/cli-reference.md` and `docs/mcp-reference.md` exist before beginning the README edit — e.g. `[ -f docs/cli-reference.md ] && [ -f docs/mcp-reference.md ] || echo 'MISSING PREREQ'` — so the implementer cannot accidentally create dead links by landing before the dependency outputs exist.
findings.finding: Both `docs/cli-reference.md` and `docs/mcp-reference.md` are asserted to exist at landing time (AC1 links to them; AC4's dead-link checker will flag them as DEAD if absent), but neither file currently exists in the repository, and the upstream tickets that create them (e866, 235a) are both still `open` with no assignee. The plan relies on these files existing as a prerequisite, but provides no AC verifying that the dependency outputs are present before the README edit proceeds.
findings.location: AC1 (`grep -qF '(docs/cli-reference.md)' README.md`); AC4 (dead-link check); 'Sequenced AFTER the CLI/MCP reference generators land'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite AC: `[ -f docs/cli-reference.md ] && [ -f docs/mcp-reference.md ] || echo 'PREREQ MISSING — land e866 and 235a first'` (prints nothing when both files exist).

findings.finding: AC2 has no inline automated proving command — it relies on a manual before/after `wc -l` comparison ('record in a comment') with no single command that fails if the threshold isn't met. An executor can satisfy this criterion by reporting numbers without the catalog actually being removed.
findings.suggested_fix: Store the pre-change line count in the plan/ticket as a literal (e.g. 'baseline: 312 lines') and add an inline command: `BEFORE=312; [ $(wc -l < README.md) -le $((BEFORE - 40)) ] || echo 'SHRINK THRESHOLD NOT MET'`

findings.finding: The Scope section requires preserving 'what/why' narrative and 'routing' sections, but AC3 only verifies `## Install` and `## Quickstart` headings — the 'what/why' and 'routing' sections have no corresponding verification, leaving those scope-required sections unchecked.
findings.location: Scope bullet 2 vs. Acceptance Criteria — AC3

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
